name: 光的波动性（干涉/衍射）
subject: physics
curriculum_points: 高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法
学业要求：能在真实情境中识别、运用「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」解决问题
活动建议：通过观察、实验、练习、讨论等方式深化对「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的理解

excerpts.text: 高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法

演示双缝干涉和单缝衍射现象，观察双缝干涉和单缝衍射图样，并以此说明光具有波的性质。
知道光的干涉、衍射和偏振现象及其应用，认识光的波动性，知道光是横波。
通过根据光的干涉、衍射等现象来论证光具有波动性，增强学生的证据意识，提升科学论证能力。

deep_snippets.text: transverse waves 416 trend line 36 twin paradox 314 U U.S. customary units 21 ultrasound 427 ultraviolet (UV) radiation 457 ultraviolet catastrophe 693 uncertainty 28 uniform circular motion 205 uniform electric field 580 universal 16 universal laws 116 up 780 V Van de Graaff 558 Van de Graaff generator 777 Vaporization 341 variables 34 vector 58, 63, 123, 254 vector addition 144 vector quantities 95 Vector subtraction 146 Velocity 63, 73, 93, 94, 99, 116, 122, 205, 254 virtual image 479 visible light 456 voltage 575 W W bosons 775 watts 282 wave 390 wave velocity 394 wavefronts 525 wavelength 394, 419, 457 weak nuclear force 772 wedge 291 weight 119, 123, 129 Werner Heisenberg 733 wheel and axle 291 work 280 work–energy theorem 281 X X-rays 457 Z Z bosons 775 zeroth law of thermodynamics 356 Index Access for free at openstax.org.

reduced and, consequently, the frequency is increased in the direction of motion, so that the observer on the right hears a higher-pitch sound. The opposite is true for the observer on the left, where the wavelength is increased and the frequency is reduced. Figure 14.16 The same effect is produced when the observers move relative to the source. Motion toward the source increases frequency as the observer on the right passes through more wave crests than she would if stationary. Motion away from the source decreases frequency as the observer on the left passes through fewer wave crests than he would if stationary. We know that wavelength and frequency are related by where v is the fixed speed of sound. The sound moves in a medium and has the same speed v in that medium whether the source is moving or not. Therefore, f multiplied by is a constant. Because the observer on the right in Figure 14.15 receives a shorter wavelength, the frequency she perceives must be higher. Similarly, the observer on the left receives a longer wavelength and therefore perceives a lower frequency. The same thing happens in Figure 14.16. A higher frequency is perceived by the observer moving toward the source, and a lower frequency is perceived by an observer moving away from the source. In general, then, relative motion of source and observer toward one another increases the perceived frequency. Relative motion apart decreases the perceived frequency. The greater the relative speed is, the greater the effect.
Introduction to the Doppler Effect This video explains the Doppler effect visually. Clic

![Figure 127 - A large illustration or diagram (952×619px) related to: Proc](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_LabManual_images/img_0127_7fb0adb9.jpg) Data and Observations Table 10.3: Waves in a Tank Angle of Card Sketch of Waves in Tray Description of Motion Parallel to wall Through a slit At 45° angle to wall Assessments 1. As the waves move across the tray and reflect off the wall, do they keep the same speed or change speed? If the speed decreased, what would happen to the wavelength if the frequency remained the same? TEKS (7)(D) 2. As the waves reflect off the wall, describe what happens to the amplitude of the wave when a. The crest of the reflected wave crosses the crest of the oncoming wave. b. The crest of the reflected wave crosses the trough of the oncoming wave.

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 光的波动性（干涉/衍射）

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 12
- **章节/领域**: 机械振动与波、光学
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法
> 学业要求：能在真实情境中识别、运用「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」解决问题
> 活动建议：通过观察、实验、练习、讨论等方式深化对「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的理解

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系工程、能源、交通或日常生活中的相关现象，讨论「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的应用边界。
- 补充科学史或技术应用案例，帮助学生理解物理规律如何服务真实问题。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
光的波动性（干涉/衍射）是物理学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以可观察实验或生活现象引入「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 探究建构：强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「光的波动性（干涉/衍射）」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

> **[Figure 319, Page 539]** A tall chart or diagram (299×623px) related to: wavelength, vacuum, medium, index, refraction, follows. This image appears on page 539 of the textbook. ![Figure 319 - A tall chart or diagram (299×623px) related to: wavelength, ](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0319_c8551e7f.png) wave characteristic that occurs for all types of waves. If diffraction is observed for a phenomenon, it is evidence that the phenomenon is produced by waves. Thus, the horizontal diffraction of the laser beam after it passes through slits in Figure 17.2 is evidence that light has the properties of a wave. Figure 17.5 Huygens’s principle applied to a straight wavefront striking an opening. The edges of the wavefront bend after passing through the opening, a process called diffraction. The amount of bending is more extreme for a small opening, consistent with the fact that wave characteristics are most noticeable for interactions with objects about the same size as the wavelength. Once again, water waves present a familiar example of a wave phenomenon that is easy to observe and understand, as shown in Figure 17.6. Figure 17.6 Ocean waves pass through an opening in a reef, resulting in a diffraction pattern. Diffraction occurs because the opening is similar in width to the wavelength of the waves.
